title: The Twelfth Point
tagline: RUMBLE left one node running. ELDER-7 knows why. 39 days to seal COLOSSUS. One active transmission that could wake it early.
villain_mandate: RUMBLE is not present physically but his active node is his presence. He has a specific reason for leaving it running — it's not an oversight. ELDER-7 knows the reason from the original sealing. The revelation of why changes the team's understanding of RUMBLE's final objective.

characters.elder_7_role: The key character this episode. He knows the original sealing architecture. He knows what an active node in a completed array does over time: it slowly degrades the dormancy field. It's not a weapon — it's a clock. RUMBLE wants COLOSSUS awake again, specifically during the permanent sealing attempt, when the team is at maximum commitment and cannot disengage.
characters.rumble_presence: One active transmission from the central plaza node. No direct appearance until scene 9.

scenes.title: Why He Left It
scenes.narration: NOVA ran the active node's transmission pattern for six hours. At hour six she had the answer: it wasn't broadcasting to anything. It was degrading the dormancy field — slowly, precisely, at a rate that would fully destabilize COLOSSUS's dormancy in thirty-nine days. Not coincidentally the same window as the permanent sealing timeline. ELDER-7 heard this and said: 'He wants it awake during your sealing attempt.' BLAZE said: 'Why?' ELDER-7 said: 'Because the original team failed their sealing attempt for that exact reason.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA's analysis showing the node transmission pattern — a slow degradation curve, timeline exactly matching the sealing window. ELDER-7's quiet recognition. The original sealing failure: shown briefly in ELDER-7's memory display — an attempt disrupted by COLOSSUS waking mid-process. BLAZE's expression: he was there. RUMBLE studied the original records. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Happened the First Time
scenes.narration: ELDER-7 told them. Three thousand years ago, the original sealing team had nearly finished the permanent array when COLOSSUS stirred prematurely. Three of the four original anchors held. The fourth team — at the eastern point — had to disengage to deal with COLOSSUS directly. The array was never completed. A temporary dormancy field was deployed instead. That's why the original seal had degraded — it was never the permanent solution. RUMBLE had found this in the historical records.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. ELDER-7 projecting ancient records — a three-thousand-year-old account of the failed sealing, showing four teams at four points, three holding, one disengaging to fight COLOSSUS. Array incomplete. Temporary field deployed. The records showing exactly what RUMBLE studied. 16:9.

scenes.title: Can We Destroy the Node
scenes.narration: NOVA proposed destroying the twelfth node — cutting the degradation signal. ELDER-7 said: 'If you destroy the node while the array is active, the backlash will accelerate the degradation. COLOSSUS wakes in hours, not days.' STORM said: 'Can we deactivate it carefully?' ELDER-7 said: 'The original team tried. The node is self-sealing against deactivation attempts — it was designed to be.' BLAZE said: 'RUMBLE designed it the same way.' ELDER-7: 'He read the original specifications.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA's deactivation proposal shot down. ELDER-7 showing the backlash diagram — destroying the node accelerates the degradation. STORM's deactivation attempt: same result. ELDER-7 pointing to the original design spec showing self-sealing architecture. RUMBLE copied it exactly. The team facing a countdown they can't stop and a process they can't interrupt. 16:9.

scenes.title: What GRANITE Asks
scenes.narration: GRANITE had been quiet. He said: 'The original fourth team — the one that disengaged. What happened to them?' ELDER-7 said: 'They held COLOSSUS alone for eleven hours while the other three teams attempted to complete the array.' GRANITE said: 'Did they succeed?' ELDER-7 said: 'No. COLOSSUS was too active. They had to abandon the attempt entirely.' GRANITE looked at his reinforcement sleeve. It was at fifty-one percent. He didn't say anything else.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. GRANITE asking the question. ELDER-7 answering: the original fourth team held alone for eleven hours. The attempt still failed. GRANITE looking at his sleeve indicator: 51%. Something calculating behind his expression. The team watching him think. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Difference
scenes.narration: NOVA said: 'The difference between then and now: the original fourth team had no information about COLOSSUS's dormancy frequency before the attempt. We do. If COLOSSUS stirs during our sealing attempt, we have the anchors, we have ELDER-7's frequency, and we have the dormancy lullaby already calibrated.' ELDER-7 said: 'You're proposing to proceed knowing it will wake.' NOVA said: 'I'm proposing to be ready for it.' ELDER-7 was quiet for a long time. Then: 'That is a better plan than we had.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA making the case — original team went in without dormancy frequency knowledge. They have it. Split screen: original team's empty hands vs. NOVA's calibrated anchor specs. ELDER-7 listening. His long pause. His acknowledgment. The original failure reframed: not a doom — a data gap that no longer exists. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Modified Sealing
scenes.narration: The plan: proceed with permanent sealing on schedule. At the moment COLOSSUS stirs — which NOVA could predict within a two-hour window based on degradation rate — deploy the dormancy lullaby anchors simultaneously with the permanent seal. Four teams at four positions, all holding. Nobody disengages. ELDER-7 said: 'The original fourth position was the one that failed. It requires the most physical endurance.' He looked at GRANITE. He did not say GRANITE's name. He looked at him.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. The plan laid out — four positions on the map, permanent seal AND dormancy lullaby running simultaneously. ELDER-7 looking at the fourth position on the map. Then at GRANITE. Not saying his name. GRANITE meeting his eyes. The assignment understood. 16:9.

scenes.title: RUMBLE Updates His Model
scenes.narration: RUMBLE's surveillance drones had picked up the team's planning session — not the specifics, but the duration and energy patterns. Six hours of continuous planning. He updated his model: they had identified the node problem and developed a countermeasure in under twelve hours. He had expected twenty-four. He added a note: ADAPTATION RATE HIGHER THAN MODELED. He looked at the sealing timeline. He looked at COLOSSUS's dormancy curve. He made one adjustment. The window he had designed got smaller. On purpose.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RUMBLE updating his model — energy pattern data from the team's session, recalculating. ADAPTATION RATE HIGHER THAN MODELED added to his log. He adjusts COLOSSUS's degradation rate slightly — making the window tighter. Not to destroy the team. To pressure-test them. He's still studying. Still designing the exam. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Night Before
scenes.narration: Thirty-one hours before the sealing attempt, NOVA ran a final check on every system. STORM ran her perimeter circuits. ELDER-7 reviewed his three-thousand-year-old memories of the original attempt, adding to his notes what he would have done differently. GRANITE sat in the repair bay — not recovering, just sitting. His sleeve at fifty-three percent. BLAZE found him there. He sat down too. They didn't talk about the plan. They talked about something else. Whatever it was, it helped.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. QUIET PRE-FINALE SCENE — NOVA checking systems, STORM running circuits, ELDER-7 writing notes by old memory, GRANITE sitting in the repair bay with sleeve at 53%. BLAZE finding him and sitting beside him. Not discussing the plan. Just present. The countdown behind them: 31:00:00. 16:9.

scenes.title: RUMBLE's Final Message
scenes.narration: At hour twenty-nine of the countdown, NOVA detected something: a direct transmission from RUMBLE to the team's frequency. First time he had ever communicated directly. She put it through. RUMBLE's voice said four words: 'I'll be watching. Good luck.' ELDER-7 looked at the communication log. He said: 'In three thousand years, I have never heard a villain say good luck.' BLAZE said: 'He means it.' NOVA said: 'I know.' Episode 12.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RUMBLE's direct transmission coming through — first ever direct communication. The team hearing his four words. ELDER-7's reaction: in 3,000 years, never. BLAZE: he means it. The transmission ends. The countdown: 29:00:00. Something about this is more unsettling than any threat. 16:9.

youtube_title: RUMBLE Left a TRAP in the Array... 😰 | Mech Legends Episode 11
next_episode_preview: Episode 12 — The Permanent Seal. Everything they've learned. Everything they've built. One attempt. COLOSSUS wakes on schedule. RUMBLE watches.
